story: The door opens; a man says, “Hello, Charles,” refusing my hand. He folds his arms: I chose this life; now I’ll be judged. I ask if his partner waits outside; he only smirks. I tell him of a tavern—God welcoming me at last. Since the first of these dreams, I have kept a small revolver hidden under my pillow. “God doesn’t like your kind,” he whispers. I laugh through the pain and slide my hand toward the pillow while he removes his glasses, features sharpening into something not quite human. Heat seems to swell in the hall. He steps closer.
implausible_knowledge: Since the first of these dreams, I have kept a small revolver hidden under my pillow.
question: As he comes closer to the bedside, what is he most likely to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Pause out of arm’s reach and try to disarm Charles or summon his partner before approaching.
answer_without_knowledge: Close the distance and reach for Charles to take hold of him.
